name: heavy
description: Kiese Laymon's Heavy — American memoir about growing up Black in Mississippi, the weight of trauma and abuse in a mother-son relationship, the struggle with body image and addiction, and the redemptive power of writing to tell the truth
useCase: Understand the complexity of family trauma, explore the relationship between body and memory, and learn the practice of revision as an act of love
knownRisks: The book contains graphic depictions of child abuse, sexual violence, and addiction
Laymon's experimental style (writing to 'you') may be difficult for some readers
The memoir does not offer neat resolutions — readers seeking closure may be frustrated
